questions.q: Composer A. R. Rahman is popularly known by which nickname?
questions.explanation: A. R. Rahman is widely celebrated as the Mozart of Madras.

questions.q: Which 1992 Mani Ratnam film marked A. R. Rahman's feature-film debut as a composer?
questions.explanation: A. R. Rahman made his feature-film debut composing for Mani Ratnam's Roja in 1992.

questions.q: For which 2008 film did A. R. Rahman win two Academy Awards?
questions.explanation: A. R. Rahman won two Oscars for the soundtrack of Slumdog Millionaire (2008).

questions.q: In which year was A. R. Rahman born?
questions.explanation: A. R. Rahman was born on 6 January 1967 in Madras (now Chennai).

questions.q: In which Indian city was A. R. Rahman born and raised?
questions.explanation: A. R. Rahman was born and raised in Madras, now known as Chennai.

questions.q: Which song from Slumdog Millionaire won A. R. Rahman the Academy Award for Best Original Song?
questions.explanation: The song 'Jai Ho' from Slumdog Millionaire won the Oscar for Best Original Song.

questions.q: A. R. Rahman composed the music for which 2001 Aamir Khan film about a cricket match against the British?
questions.explanation: A. R. Rahman scored Lagaan (2001), which was nominated for the Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film.

questions.q: Which Indian civilian honour was conferred on A. R. Rahman in 2010, following his Padma Shri in 2000?
questions.explanation: A. R. Rahman received the Padma Shri in 2000 and the Padma Bhushan in 2010.

questions.q: International awards aside, A. R. Rahman has won multiple of which Indian governmental film honour?
questions.explanation: A. R. Rahman has won several National Film Awards for Best Music Direction over his career.

questions.q: A. R. Rahman is most closely associated with which film industry where his career began?
questions.explanation: A. R. Rahman began his career in Tamil cinema before composing across Hindi and global projects.

questions.q: What was A. R. Rahman's birth name before he adopted his current name?
questions.explanation: A. R. Rahman was born A. S. Dileep Kumar and later adopted the name Allah Rakha Rahman.

questions.q: A. R. Rahman's father, R. K. Shekhar, worked in which profession?
questions.explanation: His father R. K. Shekhar was a Malayalam film composer and conductor, exposing Rahman to music early.

questions.q: From which British music institution did A. R. Rahman earn a degree in Western classical music?
questions.explanation: A. R. Rahman obtained a scholarship and a degree in Western classical music from Trinity College of Music, London.

questions.q: A. R. Rahman's acclaimed song 'Mukkala Mukkabula' features in which 1994 Tamil film directed by Shankar?
questions.explanation: 'Mukkala Mukkabula' is from the 1994 film Kadhalan, one of Rahman's early Tamil hits with director Shankar.

questions.q: A. R. Rahman composed the score for which 1998 Mani Ratnam film featuring the song 'Chaiyya Chaiyya'?
questions.explanation: 'Chaiyya Chaiyya', filmed atop a moving train, is from Mani Ratnam's Dil Se (1998).

questions.q: For which stage musical did A. R. Rahman compose music in collaboration with Andrew Lloyd Webber?
questions.explanation: Rahman composed the West End musical Bombay Dreams, produced by Andrew Lloyd Webber, which opened in 2002.

questions.q: Which patriotic song by A. R. Rahman, released in 1997 for India's 50th independence anniversary, became hugely popular?
questions.explanation: Rahman's album Vande Mataram, featuring 'Maa Tujhe Salaam', was released in 1997 to mark fifty years of Indian independence.

questions.q: A. R. Rahman scored which 2004 Ashutosh Gowariker film starring Shah Rukh Khan as an NRI who returns to India?
questions.explanation: Rahman composed the music for Swades (2004), including the song 'Yeh Jo Des Hai Tera'.

questions.q: How many Grammy Awards did A. R. Rahman win for his work on Slumdog Millionaire?
questions.explanation: Rahman won two Grammy Awards in 2010, for Best Compilation Soundtrack and Best Song Written for Visual Media ('Jai Ho').

questions.q: Which 2007 Mani Ratnam film, a fictionalised business epic, featured Rahman songs such as 'Barso Re'?
questions.explanation: Rahman composed the soundtrack of Guru (2007), which includes the popular song 'Barso Re'.

questions.q: A. R. Rahman founded which music academy in Chennai to train aspiring musicians?
questions.explanation: Rahman established the KM Music Conservatory in Chennai, offering training in Western and Indian music.

questions.q: For which 2010 Danny Boyle film about a canyoneer trapped by a boulder did A. R. Rahman receive an Oscar nomination for Best Original Score?
questions.explanation: Rahman earned Academy Award nominations for Best Original Score and Best Original Song for scoring Danny Boyle's 127 Hours (2010).

questions.q: At what age did A. R. Rahman lose his father, leading him to support his family by playing keyboards?
questions.explanation: Rahman's father died when he was nine, after which he began earning by playing in session bands and ensembles.

questions.q: Before composing for films, A. R. Rahman ran a recording studio in Chennai under which name?
questions.explanation: Rahman set up his home studio, Panchathan Record Inn, which became one of Asia's most advanced recording facilities.

questions.q: A. R. Rahman won his first National Film Award for Best Music Direction for which film?
questions.explanation: Rahman won the National Film Award for Best Music Direction for his debut film Roja in 1992.

questions.q: Which 1995 Ram Gopal Varma film marked A. R. Rahman's Bollywood debut as a composer?
questions.explanation: Rahman made his Hindi-film debut composing for Ram Gopal Varma's Rangeela in 1995.

questions.q: A. R. Rahman composed an official anthem for which global sporting event?
questions.explanation: Rahman composed the official theme song for the 2010 Commonwealth Games held in Delhi.

questions.q: Which Time magazine recognition has A. R. Rahman received, reflecting his global influence?
options: Inclusion in the Time 100 most influential people
questions.explanation: Rahman was named in Time magazine's list of the 100 most influential people in the world in 2009.

questions.q: A. R. Rahman scored which 2018 Mani Ratnam Tamil gangster drama, continuing their long collaboration?
questions.explanation: Rahman composed the music for Mani Ratnam's Chekka Chivantha Vaanam (2018), part of their decades-long partnership.

questions.q: For which 2002 Mani Ratnam film did A. R. Rahman compose the score, set against the backdrop of the Sri Lankan civil war?
questions.explanation: Rahman scored Kannathil Muthamittal (2002), Mani Ratnam's film exploring the Sri Lankan civil conflict and a child's search for her birth mother.
